steps.title: Identify the right triangle and apply the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
The right angle is marked at the bottom-left corner. The two legs are 9 (vertical) and 6 (horizontal), and $c$ is the hypotenuse. By the Pythagorean theorem:
$$c^2 = 9^2 + 6^2$$

steps.title: Calculate the sum and solve for c
steps.content:
$$c^2 = 81 + 36 = 117$$
$$c = \sqrt{117}$$
To approximate $\sqrt{117}$: since $10^2 = 100$ and $11^2 = 121$, we know $\sqrt{117}$ is between 10 and 11, closer to 11. More precisely, $10.8^2 = 116.64$, which is very close to 117. That matches choice B — B is correct.
